message:
feat(ast,parser): support schema-qualified TableStar (schema.table.*) result columns

SQLite accepts `SELECT schema.table.* FROM schema.table` — a
schema-qualified variant of the existing `table.*` result column.
FrankenSQLite's AST modeled TableStar as a bare String, which
parsed `aux.t1.*` as a single dotted identifier ("aux.t1") and
dropped the schema information on the floor.  The parser also
only recognized the two-token lookahead `id . *`, so the three-
token `id . id . *` shape failed outright.

This commit threads schema qualification through the AST,
display, and parser layers so the core connection path (in a
sibling commit) can do real schema lookup when resolving a
schema-qualified TableStar back into a column list.

crates/fsqlite-ast/src/lib.rs

- ResultColumn::TableStar(String) → ResultColumn::TableStar(QualifiedName).
  QualifiedName already exists in this crate with the shape
  `{ schema: Option<String>, name: String }`, so this is the
  natural way to carry the (optional) schema + table pair without
  adding a new wrapper type.
- test_result_column_variants: update the fixture from
  `ResultColumn::TableStar("t1".to_owned())` to
  `ResultColumn::TableStar(QualifiedName::bare("t1"))`.

crates/fsqlite-ast/src/display.rs

- fmt::Display for ResultColumn::TableStar(t): switch from
  write_ident(f, t) to write_qualified_name(f, t).  The qualified
  writer already handles the `schema.name` formatting (or bare
  `name` when schema is None), so the Display output is now the
  exact round-trip for whatever the parser accepted.  Before this
  commit, round-tripping `SELECT aux.t1.* ...` would have written
  `aux.t1` as a single identifier and lost the dotted form.

crates/fsqlite-parser/src/parser.rs

- parse_result_column() now recognizes three token shapes that
  all produce ResultColumn::TableStar:
    *                        → ResultColumn::Star
    id . *                   → ResultColumn::TableStar(bare name)
    id . id . *              → ResultColumn::TableStar(schema.name)
  The new lookahead is:
    matches!(peek,       Id | QuotedId)
    peek_nth(1) == Dot
    && (peek_nth(2) == Star
        || (matches!(peek_nth(2), Id | QuotedId)
            && peek_nth(3) == Dot
            && peek_nth(4) == Star))
  so we only commit to the TableStar branch when the whole
  `[schema .] table . *` shape is present in the token stream.
- Replace the `self.parse_identifier()` call with
  `self.parse_qualified_name()` so both shapes parse into the
  canonical QualifiedName representation, then advance() twice
  for the trailing `. *` pair.
- Existing test_select_table_star: update the assertion from
    `t == "t1"` (String comparison)
  to
    `t == &QualifiedName::bare("t1")` (QualifiedName match)
- New test_select_schema_table_star exercises the new shape:
    parse "SELECT aux.t1.* FROM aux.t1"
  and assert columns[0] matches
    ResultColumn::TableStar(QualifiedName::qualified("aux", "t1"))

No behavior change for existing `SELECT table.* FROM table`
queries — those still produce a bare-name QualifiedName and
round-trip identically.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
